ignorePatterns:
node_modules/**
dist/**
coverage/**
MODULE.bazel.lock
mise.lock
pnpm-lock.yaml
layout/src/layout-bundle.ts
**/*.generated.*

rules.rule: When a PR changes architecture, pipeline stages, agent actions, model roles, runtime configuration, BPMN rule behavior, build/test commands, prompt behavior, or GitHub Actions behavior, check the documentation files referenced in .greptile/files.json. Flag the PR if relevant documentation is stale, contradicted by the code, or missing an update.

If an Embabel @Agent, @Action, @AchievesGoal, action count, role, or action chaining behavior changes, docs/agents.md must be updated in the same PR or the PR must clearly explain why no documentation update is needed.

If pipeline stage order, typed handoffs, repair loop behavior, validation responsibilities, layout behavior, alignment behavior, or failure modes change, docs/pipeline-architecture.md and docs/goap-lifecycle.md must stay consistent with the implementation.
If model roles, Spring profiles, runtime properties, provider setup, CLI flags, run commands, or test commands change, README.md and docs/operator-guide.md must be checked and updated if affected.

If BPMN rule schema, Pkl profiles, primitive checks, rule metadata, repair capability mapping, or rule authoring workflow changes, linter/docs/rule-authoring-guide.md and linter/docs/toolbox.md must stay accurate.

The sealed interfaces in this file (ContractActivity, ContractEndState, ContractBranch, ContractTrigger, ContractIntermediateThrow) and their subtypes are internal domain types — never passed to Embabel's creating() or createObject(), never Jakarta-validated by the framework, never deserialized in production. The LLM-facing schema surface is exclusively FlatContractTypes.kt. Flag any PR that adds @get:JsonPropertyDescription, @field:NotBlank, @field:Size, or @field:Valid to a property of one of these sealed-interface subtypes. Those annotations are vestigial duplicates of the ones on the corresponding Flat DTO and re-introduce the cross-variant copy-paste that //:cpd_kotlin_main detects (issue #343).

When reviewing tests that generate or validate BPMN XML, do not allow string-based assertions like `assertContains(xml, "<tag>")` or `xml.contains("attr")`. All structural XML validation must use `XMLUnit` XPath assertions via `assertXml(xml).nodesByXPath(...)` or `assertXml(xml).valueByXPath(...)`. Fail the review if legacy string-matching is introduced for XML payloads.

ContractVocabularySmokeTest (src/test/kotlin/dev/groknull/bpmner/smoke/ContractVocabularySmokeTest.kt) is the live-LLM guard that every contract-vocabulary construct is actually extractable from natural-language prose. If this PR adds a new vocabulary construct — a new ContractActivity / ContractEndState / ContractBranch / ContractTrigger / ContractIntermediateThrow subtype, a new FlatActivityKind / FlatEndStateKind / BoundaryEventKind / ContractArtifactKind / ContractGatewayKind enum value, or a new cross-cutting construct surfaced from prose (actor responsibility, iteration, standard loop, data artifact, pool/lane, message flow) — flag the PR unless it also adds or extends a ContractVocabularySmokeTest case that exercises that construct end-to-end, or clearly explains why a live-extraction case is not applicable.
